title: PDF Monkey API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and key terms for the PDF Monkey PDF generation API.

terms.definition: A generated or in-progress PDF file created by rendering a DocumentTemplate with a specific JSON payload. Documents have a lifecycle status (draft, pending, generating, success, failure).
A lightweight representation of a Document that excludes large fields like payload and generation_logs. Used for listing and polling generation status efficiently.
A reusable template that defines the HTML/Liquid body, SCSS styles, sample data, and PDF settings used to generate documents. Templates support both code editor and visual builder modes.
A lightweight representation of a DocumentTemplate used in listing endpoints. Excludes body, styles, and settings content.
The JSON data object passed when creating a document that is merged with the template body during generation. The template uses Handlebars/Liquid syntax to reference payload values.
Custom metadata object attached to a document for application-level tracking (e.g., order IDs, customer IDs). Maximum size is 200 KB. Not used in PDF rendering.

The current lifecycle state of a document. Values: draft (not yet queued), pending (queued for generation), generating (actively being processed), success (completed successfully), failure (generation failed).
A temporary signed URL pointing to the generated PDF on Amazon S3. Valid for 1 hour from generation. Only available when document status is 'success'.
The template editing interface mode. 'code' uses the HTML/Liquid/SCSS code editor; 'builder' uses a visual drag-and-drop interface.
The format of the generated file. 'pdf' produces a PDF document; 'image' produces a rasterized image.
PDF generation configuration on a template controlling page dimensions, margins, orientation, headers, footers, and rendering options.
The standard page size for generated PDFs. Supported: a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, letter, custom. Use 'custom' with paper_height and paper_width for non-standard sizes.
Page orientation of the generated PDF. Either 'portrait' (vertical) or 'landscape' (horizontal).
Time-to-live in seconds set on a template. Determines how long generated documents and their download URLs are retained before automatic deletion.
An organizational container (also called 'app') grouping templates and documents. Identified by app_id in the API.
An organizational folder within a workspace used to group related document templates.
A human-readable string (1-100 chars) used to reference a template by name instead of UUID. Must be unique within a workspace.
A generation mode (via POST /documents/sync) where the API request waits until PDF generation completes before returning. Maximum wait time is 6 minutes. Returns a DocumentCard directly.
The default generation mode (via POST /documents) where the API returns immediately with status 'pending'. The client must poll or use webhooks to detect completion.
HTTP callback notifications sent by PDF Monkey (via Svix) when document generation events occur. Allows applications to react to completion without polling.
The rendering engine used to convert HTML templates into PDF files. Referenced by pdf_engine_id on templates. Engines may be deprecated over time.
The API authentication mechanism. Include the secret API key from the Dashboard as 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_SECRET_KEY' in all API requests.
An unpublished version of template content (body_draft, scss_style_draft, settings_draft). Draft fields are edited in the Dashboard and must be published to take effect in document generation.
